description: Finish the public launch of Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre by correcting the remaining public brand, contact-workflow, service-area, privacy, and launch-indexing inconsistencies without changing the locked product positioning or inventing commercial terms.
non_goals: Do not create or claim Google Business Profile, Search Console, Yelp, GigSalad, or advertising-account setup inside this repository.
Do not invent fixed travel fees, free-travel radii, package prices, testimonials, social links, business hours, or legal guarantees.
Do not redesign the visual system, final logo, wordmark artwork, or trademark-gated assets.
Do not change show titles, show content, the lead-delivery architecture, database schema, or notification providers.
Do not merge directly to main; deliver the work through a reviewable pull request.

must_not_violate: The locked public brand is Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre on misslanatheatre.com.
The primary public contact is info@misslanatheatre.com and (323) 903-2039; SMS, email, and WhatsApp are accepted and written contact is preferred.
Los Angeles is the business base, while San Diego, Sacramento, San Jose, and other California locations may be served by request.
Public pricing remains from $350; travel outside the normal local service area receives a custom quote with no invented amount or blanket free-travel promise.
Only real public routes may be indexed; /design and /api remain excluded.
No Russian or Slavic brand coding and no puppet-theater-first positioning.
allowed_change_classes: robots, sitemap, metadata, and public-route verification

forbidden_execution_patterns: No direct commit or push to main.
No replacement of the working lead pipeline with a mock or client-only success state.
No public internal labels such as placeholder, GBP-first, embed later, phase, or TODO.
No promise that Orange County, greater LA, or any named radius is free unless the owner has explicitly approved that commercial rule.
No claim that external indexing, Search Console submission, Google Business verification, or advertising profiles are complete without direct evidence.
No broad refactor while touching launch copy.
execution_guardrails: Preserve the existing production lead endpoint, durable persistence, Telegram/email/Sheets notifications, and honest success behavior.
Use the existing design-system components and tokens; do not add ad-hoc styling.
Keep contact language channel-neutral: text, email, WhatsApp, or phone, with a 1-2 business day response expectation.
Describe geography as a Los Angeles base with wider California travel by request and a custom travel quote.
Keep all public pages indexable except explicitly internal routes, and keep the sitemap synchronized with the real route tree.
Add regression tests for every corrected launch inconsistency.
Stage and review only task-owned files.

steps.title: Audit the current launch state
actions: Verify current robots, sitemap, metadata, public route set, contact copy, service-area copy, and visible brand spelling.
Separate already-completed launch work from remaining defects.
steps.expectation: The implementation changes only defects that still exist in the current main branch.

steps.title: Correct the public brand and contact workflow
actions: Render the locked possessive brand spelling in public wordmark surfaces.
Expose the primary email alongside the primary phone.
Replace call-first copy with channel-neutral response copy.
Add a clear preferred-reply choice to the booking form without weakening server-side lead validation or persistence.
steps.expectation: Visitors can choose a practical reply channel and the public UI no longer contradicts the documented workflow.

steps.title: Normalize service-area and travel language
actions: Present Los Angeles as the base and wider California travel as available by request.
Keep San Diego, Sacramento, and San Jose visible without implying they are local or free.
Align structured data with the public service-area copy.
steps.expectation: Every public surface describes geography consistently and does not invent a travel-pricing rule.

steps.title: Remove launch placeholders and add privacy transparency
actions: Replace the public service-area map placeholder with finished customer-facing content.
Add an indexable privacy page describing inquiry data use in plain language.
Link the privacy page from the footer and include it in the sitemap.
steps.expectation: No internal launch placeholder remains public and visitors can understand how inquiry data is handled.

steps.title: Verify the full public launch
actions: Run the exact CI gate and Playwright e2e suite.
Review the complete diff using BLOCKER, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, and NOTE severity.
Record substantive changes in the project progress log and produce a launch report.
steps.expectation: All checks are green, no open BLOCKER or HIGH finding remains, and the work is submitted as a pull request.

success_criteria: The public wordmark uses Miss Lana's Fairy-Tale Theatre consistently.
The footer and booking page expose info@misslanatheatre.com and (323) 903-2039.
The booking flow no longer says email is optional because the theatre will mostly call, and the visitor can state a preferred reply channel.
No public page contains GBP-first, embed later, placeholder, or other internal launch wording.
Los Angeles is identified as the base; San Diego, Sacramento, San Jose, and other California locations are available by request; no unverified blanket free-travel claim remains.
All real marketing routes, including /privacy, are indexable and listed in sitemap.xml; /design and /api remain excluded.
The lead API and durable notification pipeline continue to work without a fake success path.
Targeted tests and the exact CI gate pass.
A reviewable pull request exists and main was not directly modified.

manual_checks: Check the header and footer wordmark on desktop and mobile.
Check the footer and booking page phone/email links.
Submit the booking form through the mocked e2e endpoint with each preferred reply option.
Inspect /booking, /pricing, /school-shows, /birthdays, and Home for consistent travel wording.
Inspect /robots.txt and /sitemap.xml and confirm /privacy is included while /design and /api are excluded.
Search the product code for GBP-first, embed later, we'll mostly call, and unapproved free-travel claims.
Review the diff for unintended pricing, brand, show, or pipeline changes.

progress_updates: Append a substantive launch entry to docs/PROJECT_PROGRESS.md.
artifacts: A reviewable GitHub pull request from fix/full-launch-contact-and-seo to main.
Updated public pages, components, metadata/schema, sitemap, privacy route, and regression tests.

closure_sequence: Step 1: Scope verification — confirm every changed file is required for the launch defects and no non_goal was implemented.
Step 2: Verification evidence — run pnpm run ci:exact and pnpm test:e2e and record exact outcomes.
Step 3: Reviewer-grade diff review — classify findings as BLOCKER, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, or NOTE; do not close with an open BLOCKER or HIGH.
Step 4: Public-surface review — confirm brand, contacts, preferred reply, geography, travel, privacy, indexability, sitemap, and internal-placeholder removal on the actual rendered pages.
Step 5: Execution-safety review — confirm no non_goals were fixed, no forbidden_execution_patterns were violated, and all execution_guardrails were respected.
Step 6: Documentation — create the launch report and append the project progress entry.
Step 7: Delivery — open a pull request; do not merge or modify main without the operator's explicit approval.

post_actions: Do not claim Search Console submission, Google Business verification, or advertising-platform setup until those external actions are separately completed and verified.
After merge and deployment, verify the live robots.txt, sitemap.xml, canonical tags, lead delivery, and production contact links.
Create separate tasks for Google Business Profile, Search Console submission, marketplace profiles, reviews, and paid advertising.
